title: DHA
description: Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) is a primary omega-3 long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acid that serves as a structural component of the human brain, cerebral cortex, and retina. It modulates cell membrane fluidity and signaling pathways, playing a critical role in neurodevelopment, ocular function, and the prevention of cardiovascular and neurodegenerative diseases.

research.title: Health benefits of docosahexaenoic acid and its bioavailability: A review
research.summary: This review confirms DHA's essential role in fetal development, cardiovascular health, and the improvement of cognitive function throughout all life stages.

research.title: Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA): An essential nutrient and a nutraceutical for brain health and diseases
research.summary: DHA is identified as a critical nutrient for maintaining brain structure and function, showing potential in managing neurological disorders and improving cognitive performance.

sentiment.summary: Users overwhelmingly view DHA as a foundational health supplement, frequently citing its benefits for mental clarity, mood stabilization, and as a core component of fertility and longevity stacks.

effects.label: Long-term Consistency:
effects.description: Most users note that benefits for mood and memory require several weeks or months of consistent daily use to become apparent.

effects.label: Synergistic Potential:
effects.description: Effectiveness appears boosted when stacked with Vitamin D, K, and EPA for cardiovascular and hormonal health.

effects.label: Structural Foundation:
effects.description: Unlike acute stimulants, DHA is perceived as a 'maintenance' supplement that supports the physical structure of the brain and eyes.

effects.label: Hormonal Support:
effects.description: While not a testosterone booster on its own, it is frequently included in successful protocols for optimizing natural hormone levels.

dosage.label: Standard Daily Dose:
dosage.description: A common clinical and user recommendation is approximately 400mg to 1000mg of DHA per day.

dosage.label: Fish Oil Ratios:
dosage.description: Many users aim for a combined EPA/DHA intake, such as an 800mg EPA to 400mg DHA ratio for general wellness.

dosage.label: Fertility Protocols:
dosage.description: Higher dosages are often reported by users preparing for IVF or looking to improve reproductive health.

dosage.label: Bioavailability Focus:
dosage.description: Users recommend taking DHA with a fat-containing meal to maximize absorption.

sideEffects.label: Digestive Discomfort:
sideEffects.description: Some users report 'fishy burps' or mild indigestion, often mitigated by using enteric-coated capsules.

sideEffects.label: Blood Thinning:
sideEffects.description: At high doses, users note the potential for increased bleeding time, which may interact with certain medications.

sideEffects.label: Sensory Issues:
sideEffects.description: A small subset of users finds the smell or taste of oxidized lower-quality oils off-putting.

sideEffects.label: Brain Fog (Rare):
sideEffects.description: Occasional reports of paradoxical brain fog if the ratio between EPA and DHA is not balanced for the individual's needs.

availability.label: Over-the-Counter:
availability.description: Widely available globally as a dietary supplement in softgel or liquid form.

availability.label: Source Variation:
availability.description: Available from fish oil, krill oil, or vegan-friendly algal oil (which is the primary source for fish themselves).

availability.label: Quality Testing:
availability.description: Users emphasize sourcing from reputable brands that provide third-party testing for heavy metals and oxidation levels.
